changes.value:
## What

The Window menu's window list is empty after startup because the menu is built before any windows are visible, and no `rebuild_menu` is called after they appear.

**Root cause** — two missing `rebuild_menu` calls:

1. **`kanban-app/src/main.rs:100`** — `build_menu_from_commands` is called during `setup()` with `&[]` for windows (comment: "At startup there are no visible windows yet"). The main window is shown at ~line 145, but no `rebuild_menu` follows. The `Focused(true)` handler at ~line 216 only calls `update_window_focus_checkmarks`, which updates checkmarks on *existing* menu items — but the items don't exist yet.

2. **`kanban-app/src/commands.rs:715` (`restore_windows`)** — restores secondary windows but never calls `menu::rebuild_menu` afterward. Compare with `create_window_impl` at line 704 which correctly calls `menu::rebuild_menu(&app)`.

**Why it regresses**: any change to the startup sequence, menu build triggers, or window creation paths can silently drop the rebuild. The `Focused(true)` handler looks like it handles window menu updates, but it only flips checkmarks — it cannot add new entries.

### Files to modify
- `kanban-app/src/main.rs` — add `menu::rebuild_menu(app.handle())` after `win.show()` + `win.set_focus()` for the main window (~line 147)
- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` — add `menu::rebuild_menu(&app)` at the end of `restore_windows`, after the loop, before the `Ok(...)` return (~line 801)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] After app startup, the Window menu shows the main window with a checkmark
- [ ] After `restore_windows` creates secondary windows, all appear in the Window menu
- [ ] Clicking a window entry in the Window menu focuses it and updates the checkmark
- [ ] No visible menu flicker during startup

## Tests
- [ ] Manual: launch app, click Window menu — main window should be listed with a checkmark
- [ ] Manual: open a second window (Cmd+N), close app, relaunch — both windows should appear in the Window menu after restore
- [ ] `kanban-app/src/menu.rs` — existing `rebuild_menu` function has no unit tests; this is a Tauri-integration-level concern tested manually
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` — no regressions
Fix: Windows not appearing in Window menu (startup + restore race)

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -8,20 +8,31 @@

 2. **`kanban-app/src/commands.rs:715` (`restore_windows`)** — restores secondary windows but never calls `menu::rebuild_menu` afterward. Compare with `create_window_impl` at line 704 which correctly calls `menu::rebuild_menu(&app)`.

-**Why it regresses**: any change to the startup sequence, menu build triggers, or window creation paths can silently drop the rebuild. The `Focused(true)` handler looks like it handles window menu updates, but it only flips checkmarks — it cannot add new entries.
+## Why this keeps regressing
+
+This bug was introduced in commit `5281fe4d` (Mar 29) when menu construction moved from being **frontend-triggered after load** (where windows were already visible) to **`setup()` in `main.rs`** (before any windows exist). No compensating rebuild was added.
+
+The `Focused(true)` handler is a **red herring** — it calls `update_window_focus_checkmarks`, which looks like it handles the window menu, but it only flips checkmarks on *existing* `CheckMenuItem`s. It **cannot add new entries**. Every reviewer sees this handler and assumes the window menu is covered, so the missing rebuilds go unnoticed.
+
+The pattern: `create_window_impl` correctly calls `rebuild_menu` → reviewer assumes other window-creation paths do too → they don't.
+
+## Fix — why a simple 2-line patch isn't enough
+
+The two `rebuild_menu` calls will fix the immediate bug, but to prevent regression, `Focused(true)` should also detect when the window is missing from the menu and trigger a rebuild. This covers any future code path that creates a window without remembering to call `rebuild_menu`.

 ### Files to modify
 - `kanban-app/src/main.rs` — add `menu::rebuild_menu(app.handle())` after `win.show()` + `win.set_focus()` for the main window (~line 147)
 - `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` — add `menu::rebuild_menu(&app)` at the end of `restore_windows`, after the loop, before the `Ok(...)` return (~line 801)
+- `kanban-app/src/menu.rs` — in `update_window_focus_checkmarks`, detect if the focused window has no `window.focus:{label}` item in the menu and call `rebuild_menu` as a fallback (prevents future regressions from any code path that creates a window without rebuilding)

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] After app startup, the Window menu shows the main window with a checkmark
 - [ ] After `restore_windows` creates secondary windows, all appear in the Window menu
 - [ ] Clicking a window entry in the Window menu focuses it and updates the checkmark
+- [ ] A window created by any future code path appears in the Window menu on focus, even without an explicit `rebuild_menu` call (regression guard)
 - [ ] No visible menu flicker during startup

 ## Tests
 - [ ] Manual: launch app, click Window menu — main window should be listed with a checkmark
 - [ ] Manual: open a second window (Cmd+N), close app, relaunch — both windows should appear in the Window menu after restore
-- [ ] `kanban-app/src/menu.rs` — existing `rebuild_menu` function has no unit tests; this is a Tauri-integration-level concern tested manually
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` — no regressions
\ No newline at end of file

+**Why it regresses**: any change to the startup sequence, menu build triggers, or window creation paths can silently drop the rebuild. The `Focused(true)` handler looks like it handles window menu updates, but it only flips checkmarks — it cannot add new entries.

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,38 +1,54 @@
 ## What

-The Window menu's window list is empty after startup because the menu is built before any windows are visible, and no `rebuild_menu` is called after they appear.
+The Window menu's window list is empty after startup and regresses repeatedly because `rebuild_menu` calls are sprinkled at individual window-creation sites instead of being tied to the real invariant: **focus changes require a menu rebuild.**

-**Root cause** — two missing `rebuild_menu` calls:
+When the focused window changes, three things change simultaneously:
+1. The scope chain root (`window:{label}`) — different commands are available
+2. The window list checkmarks — different window is active
+3. Command enabled states — scope-dependent availability shifts

-1. **`kanban-app/src/main.rs:100`** — `build_menu_from_commands` is called during `setup()` with `&[]` for windows (comment: "At startup there are no visible windows yet"). The main window is shown at ~line 145, but no `rebuild_menu` follows. The `Focused(true)` handler at ~line 216 only calls `update_window_focus_checkmarks`, which updates checkmarks on *existing* menu items — but the items don't exist yet.
+All three require a full menu rebuild. And every window creation/switch already calls `set_focus()`, which fires `Focused(true)`.

-2. **`kanban-app/src/commands.rs:715` (`restore_windows`)** — restores secondary windows but never calls `menu::rebuild_menu` afterward. Compare with `create_window_impl` at line 704 which correctly calls `menu::rebuild_menu(&app)`.
+## Root cause

-## Why this keeps regressing
+`Focused(true)` calls `update_window_focus_checkmarks` — a lightweight function that only flips checkmarks on *existing* menu items. It cannot add new items or update command availability. This was a premature optimization that broke the contract: focus changes must rebuild the menu.

-This bug was introduced in commit `5281fe4d` (Mar 29) when menu construction moved from being **frontend-triggered after load** (where windows were already visible) to **`setup()` in `main.rs`** (before any windows exist). No compensating rebuild was added.
+## Fix

-The `Focused(true)` handler is a **red herring** — it calls `update_window_focus_checkmarks`, which looks like it handles the window menu, but it only flips checkmarks on *existing* `CheckMenuItem`s. It **cannot add new entries**. Every reviewer sees this handler and assumes the window menu is covered, so the missing rebuilds go unnoticed.
+**`Focused(true)` should call `rebuild_menu`, not `update_window_focus_checkmarks`.**

-The pattern: `create_window_impl` correctly calls `rebuild_menu` → reviewer assumes other window-creation paths do too → they don't.
+This single change covers every case:
+- Startup: main window gets focus after `show()` → rebuild adds it to Window menu
+- `restore_windows`: last restored window gets `set_focus()` → rebuild adds all windows
+- `create_window_impl`: already calls `set_focus()` → rebuild adds the new window
+- Alt-tabbing between windows → rebuild updates checkmarks + command availability
+- `window.focus:` menu clicks → already call `set_focus()` → rebuild updates checkmarks

-## Fix — why a simple 2-line patch isn't enough
+After this change, the explicit `rebuild_menu` call in `create_window_impl` becomes redundant (focus event handles it). Keep the `Destroyed` handler's `rebuild_menu` — a destroyed window doesn't trigger focus on the remaining window in all cases.

-The two `rebuild_menu` calls will fix the immediate bug, but to prevent regression, `Focused(true)` should also detect when the window is missing from the menu and trigger a rebuild. This covers any future code path that creates a window without remembering to call `rebuild_menu`.
+Skip `quick-capture` window — it already returns early from `Focused(true)`.

 ### Files to modify
-- `kanban-app/src/main.rs` — add `menu::rebuild_menu(app.handle())` after `win.show()` + `win.set_focus()` for the main window (~line 147)
-- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` — add `menu::rebuild_menu(&app)` at the end of `restore_windows`, after the loop, before the `Ok(...)` return (~line 801)
-- `kanban-app/src/menu.rs` — in `update_window_focus_checkmarks`, detect if the focused window has no `window.focus:{label}` item in the menu and call `rebuild_menu` as a fallback (prevents future regressions from any code path that creates a window without rebuilding)
+- `kanban-app/src/main.rs` (~line 220) — change `update_window_focus_checkmarks` to `rebuild_menu` in `Focused(true)` handler
+- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` (~line 699) — optionally remove redundant `rebuild_menu` from `create_window_impl` (focus event handles it now)
+- `kanban-app/src/menu.rs` — `update_window_focus_checkmarks` can be removed or kept for reference
+
+## Why this won't regress again
+
+The previous approach required every code path that creates a window to remember to call `rebuild_menu`. The new approach ties the rebuild to the OS focus event — which is unavoidable. You can't create, switch, or restore a window without it receiving focus. No call site to forget.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] After app startup, the Window menu shows the main window with a checkmark
-- [ ] After `restore_windows` creates secondary windows, all appear in the Window menu
-- [ ] Clicking a window entry in the Window menu focuses it and updates the checkmark
-- [ ] A window created by any future code path appears in the Window menu on focus, even without an explicit `rebuild_menu` call (regression guard)
-- [ ] No visible menu flicker during startup
+- [ ] After `restore_windows`, all windows appear in the Window menu
+- [ ] New window (Cmd+N) appears in the Window menu immediately
+- [ ] Closing a secondary window removes it from the Window menu
+- [ ] Alt-tabbing between windows updates the checkmark
+- [ ] Quick-capture toggle does not cause unnecessary menu rebuilds
+- [ ] No visible menu flicker during normal use

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Manual: launch app, click Window menu — main window should be listed with a checkmark
-- [ ] Manual: open a second window (Cmd+N), close app, relaunch — both windows should appear in the Window menu after restore
+- [ ] Manual: launch app → Window menu lists main window with checkmark
+- [ ] Manual: Cmd+N → new window appears in Window menu
+- [ ] Manual: close secondary window → removed from Window menu
+- [ ] Manual: quit and relaunch with multiple windows → all restored windows in Window menu
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` — no regressions
\ No newline at end of file

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,41 +1,39 @@
 ## What

-The Window menu's window list is empty after startup and regresses repeatedly because `rebuild_menu` calls are sprinkled at individual window-creation sites instead of being tied to the real invariant: **focus changes require a menu rebuild.**
-
-When the focused window changes, three things change simultaneously:
-1. The scope chain root (`window:{label}`) — different commands are available
-2. The window list checkmarks — different window is active
-3. Command enabled states — scope-dependent availability shifts
-
-All three require a full menu rebuild. And every window creation/switch already calls `set_focus()`, which fires `Focused(true)`.
+The Window menu's window list is empty after startup and keeps regressing because menu rebuilds are scattered across OS event handlers instead of flowing through the single focus/scope chain system.

 ## Root cause

-`Focused(true)` calls `update_window_focus_checkmarks` — a lightweight function that only flips checkmarks on *existing* menu items. It cannot add new items or update command availability. This was a premature optimization that broke the contract: focus changes must rebuild the menu.
+Menu updates are handled by three separate mechanisms with no unifying invariant:
+1. `rebuild_menu` — called ad-hoc from `create_window_impl`, `Destroyed`, `BoardSwitch`, keymap changes
+2. `update_menu_enabled_state` — called after every `dispatch_command_internal`, toggles enabled/disabled only
+3. `update_window_focus_checkmarks` — called from OS `Focused(true)`, flips checkmarks only

-## Fix
+None of these are tied to the thing that actually determines what the menu should show: **the scope chain**. The scope chain changes on every focus change — clicking a card, a tag, a column, or switching windows. It includes the `window:{label}` root. It determines available commands. The menu should be a pure function of the scope chain.

-**`Focused(true)` should call `rebuild_menu`, not `update_window_focus_checkmarks`.**
+## Fix

-This single change covers every case:
-- Startup: main window gets focus after `show()` → rebuild adds it to Window menu
-- `restore_windows`: last restored window gets `set_focus()` → rebuild adds all windows
-- `create_window_impl`: already calls `set_focus()` → rebuild adds the new window
-- Alt-tabbing between windows → rebuild updates checkmarks + command availability
-- `window.focus:` menu clicks → already call `set_focus()` → rebuild updates checkmarks
+**`rebuild_menu` should run on `ui.setFocus` — the only event that matters.**

-After this change, the explicit `rebuild_menu` call in `create_window_impl` becomes redundant (focus event handles it). Keep the `Destroyed` handler's `rebuild_menu` — a destroyed window doesn't trigger focus on the remaining window in all cases.
+In `dispatch_command_internal`, when `ui.setFocus` is dispatched, call `rebuild_menu` instead of relying on `update_menu_enabled_state` alone. This covers every case through one path:

-Skip `quick-capture` window — it already returns early from `Focused(true)`.
+- **Startup**: frontend mounts → `CommandScopeProvider` registers `window:main` → initial focus dispatches `ui.setFocus` → `rebuild_menu` → window appears in menu
+- **New window**: `set_focus()` → frontend mounts → `ui.setFocus` with new `window:{label}` root → `rebuild_menu` → new window in menu
+- **Restore windows**: each restored window gets `set_focus()` → same flow → all windows in menu
+- **Click a card**: `FocusScope` → `setFocus("task:abc")` → `ui.setFocus` → `rebuild_menu` → menu reflects task-scoped commands
+- **Switch windows**: OS focus → frontend re-dispatches its scope chain → `ui.setFocus` → `rebuild_menu` → checkmark moves
+- **Close window**: `Destroyed` handler stays (destroyed windows don't get focus events) → `rebuild_menu` → window removed

 ### Files to modify
-- `kanban-app/src/main.rs` (~line 220) — change `update_window_focus_checkmarks` to `rebuild_menu` in `Focused(true)` handler
-- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` (~line 699) — optionally remove redundant `rebuild_menu` from `create_window_impl` (focus event handles it now)
-- `kanban-app/src/menu.rs` — `update_window_focus_checkmarks` can be removed or kept for reference
+- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` `dispatch_command_internal` — call `rebuild_menu` when `effective_cmd == "ui.setFocus"` (replaces the ad-hoc `update_menu_enabled_state` for this command)
+- `kanban-app/src/main.rs` `Focused(true)` — remove `update_window_focus_checkmarks` call; keep `most_recent_board` bookkeeping only. Menu updates flow through the scope chain, not OS events.
+- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` `create_window_impl` — remove explicit `rebuild_menu` call (focus event handles it)
+- `kanban-app/src/menu.rs` — `update_window_focus_checkmarks` can be deleted (dead code)
+- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` `dispatch_command_internal` — the existing `rebuild_menu` calls for `BoardSwitch`/`BoardClose`/keymap should stay (those change registry contents, not just scope)

-## Why this won't regress again
+## Why this won't regress

-The previous approach required every code path that creates a window to remember to call `rebuild_menu`. The new approach ties the rebuild to the OS focus event — which is unavoidable. You can't create, switch, or restore a window without it receiving focus. No call site to forget.
+Previous fixes required every code path that creates/destroys/switches a window to remember to call `rebuild_menu`. The new approach requires nothing — focus changes flow through `ui.setFocus` automatically. You can't interact with a window without focusing something in it. One system, one path, no call sites to forget.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] After app startup, the Window menu shows the main window with a checkmark
@@ -42,13 +40,14 @@
 - [ ] After `restore_windows`, all windows appear in the Window menu
 - [ ] New window (Cmd+N) appears in the Window menu immediately
 - [ ] Closing a secondary window removes it from the Window menu
-- [ ] Alt-tabbing between windows updates the checkmark
-- [ ] Quick-capture toggle does not cause unnecessary menu rebuilds
-- [ ] No visible menu flicker during normal use
+- [ ] Clicking a card updates menu item names (e.g. "Delete Task") and enabled states
+- [ ] Switching windows moves the checkmark
+- [ ] Quick-capture toggle does not cause unnecessary menu rebuilds (returns early before focus dispatch)

 ## Tests
 - [ ] Manual: launch app → Window menu lists main window with checkmark
 - [ ] Manual: Cmd+N → new window appears in Window menu
-- [ ] Manual: close secondary window → removed from Window menu
-- [ ] Manual: quit and relaunch with multiple windows → all restored windows in Window menu
+- [ ] Manual: close secondary window → removed from Window menu  
+- [ ] Manual: click a task card → Edit menu items update names/enabled state
+- [ ] Manual: quit and relaunch with multiple windows → all appear after restore
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` — no regressions
\ No newline at end of file
changes.reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,39 +1,41 @@
 ## What

-The Window menu's window list is empty after startup and keeps regressing because menu rebuilds are scattered across OS event handlers instead of flowing through the single focus/scope chain system.
+The Window menu's window list is empty after startup and regresses repeatedly because `rebuild_menu` calls are sprinkled at individual window-creation sites instead of being tied to the real invariant: **focus changes require a menu rebuild.**

-## Root cause
+When the focused window changes, three things change simultaneously:
+1. The scope chain root (`window:{label}`) — different commands are available
+2. The window list checkmarks — different window is active
+3. Command enabled states — scope-dependent availability shifts

-Menu updates are handled by three separate mechanisms with no unifying invariant:
-1. `rebuild_menu` — called ad-hoc from `create_window_impl`, `Destroyed`, `BoardSwitch`, keymap changes
-2. `update_menu_enabled_state` — called after every `dispatch_command_internal`, toggles enabled/disabled only
-3. `update_window_focus_checkmarks` — called from OS `Focused(true)`, flips checkmarks only
+All three require a full menu rebuild. And every window creation/switch already calls `set_focus()`, which fires `Focused(true)`.

-None of these are tied to the thing that actually determines what the menu should show: **the scope chain**. The scope chain changes on every focus change — clicking a card, a tag, a column, or switching windows. It includes the `window:{label}` root. It determines available commands. The menu should be a pure function of the scope chain.
+## Root cause

+`Focused(true)` calls `update_window_focus_checkmarks` — a lightweight function that only flips checkmarks on *existing* menu items. It cannot add new items or update command availability. This was a premature optimization that broke the contract: focus changes must rebuild the menu.
+
 ## Fix

-**`rebuild_menu` should run on `ui.setFocus` — the only event that matters.**
+**`Focused(true)` should call `rebuild_menu`, not `update_window_focus_checkmarks`.**

-In `dispatch_command_internal`, when `ui.setFocus` is dispatched, call `rebuild_menu` instead of relying on `update_menu_enabled_state` alone. This covers every case through one path:
+This single change covers every case:
+- Startup: main window gets focus after `show()` → rebuild adds it to Window menu
+- `restore_windows`: last restored window gets `set_focus()` → rebuild adds all windows
+- `create_window_impl`: already calls `set_focus()` → rebuild adds the new window
+- Alt-tabbing between windows → rebuild updates checkmarks + command availability
+- `window.focus:` menu clicks → already call `set_focus()` → rebuild updates checkmarks

-- **Startup**: frontend mounts → `CommandScopeProvider` registers `window:main` → initial focus dispatches `ui.setFocus` → `rebuild_menu` → window appears in menu
-- **New window**: `set_focus()` → frontend mounts → `ui.setFocus` with new `window:{label}` root → `rebuild_menu` → new window in menu
-- **Restore windows**: each restored window gets `set_focus()` → same flow → all windows in menu
-- **Click a card**: `FocusScope` → `setFocus("task:abc")` → `ui.setFocus` → `rebuild_menu` → menu reflects task-scoped commands
-- **Switch windows**: OS focus → frontend re-dispatches its scope chain → `ui.setFocus` → `rebuild_menu` → checkmark moves
-- **Close window**: `Destroyed` handler stays (destroyed windows don't get focus events) → `rebuild_menu` → window removed
+After this change, the explicit `rebuild_menu` call in `create_window_impl` becomes redundant (focus event handles it). Keep the `Destroyed` handler's `rebuild_menu` — a destroyed window doesn't trigger focus on the remaining window in all cases.

+Skip `quick-capture` window — it already returns early from `Focused(true)`.
+
 ### Files to modify
-- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` `dispatch_command_internal` — call `rebuild_menu` when `effective_cmd == "ui.setFocus"` (replaces the ad-hoc `update_menu_enabled_state` for this command)
-- `kanban-app/src/main.rs` `Focused(true)` — remove `update_window_focus_checkmarks` call; keep `most_recent_board` bookkeeping only. Menu updates flow through the scope chain, not OS events.
-- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` `create_window_impl` — remove explicit `rebuild_menu` call (focus event handles it)
-- `kanban-app/src/menu.rs` — `update_window_focus_checkmarks` can be deleted (dead code)
-- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` `dispatch_command_internal` — the existing `rebuild_menu` calls for `BoardSwitch`/`BoardClose`/keymap should stay (those change registry contents, not just scope)
+- `kanban-app/src/main.rs` (~line 220) — change `update_window_focus_checkmarks` to `rebuild_menu` in `Focused(true)` handler
+- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` (~line 699) — optionally remove redundant `rebuild_menu` from `create_window_impl` (focus event handles it now)
+- `kanban-app/src/menu.rs` — `update_window_focus_checkmarks` can be removed or kept for reference

-## Why this won't regress
+## Why this won't regress again

-Previous fixes required every code path that creates/destroys/switches a window to remember to call `rebuild_menu`. The new approach requires nothing — focus changes flow through `ui.setFocus` automatically. You can't interact with a window without focusing something in it. One system, one path, no call sites to forget.
+The previous approach required every code path that creates a window to remember to call `rebuild_menu`. The new approach ties the rebuild to the OS focus event — which is unavoidable. You can't create, switch, or restore a window without it receiving focus. No call site to forget.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] After app startup, the Window menu shows the main window with a checkmark
@@ -40,14 +42,13 @@
 - [ ] After `restore_windows`, all windows appear in the Window menu
 - [ ] New window (Cmd+N) appears in the Window menu immediately
 - [ ] Closing a secondary window removes it from the Window menu
-- [ ] Clicking a card updates menu item names (e.g. "Delete Task") and enabled states
-- [ ] Switching windows moves the checkmark
-- [ ] Quick-capture toggle does not cause unnecessary menu rebuilds (returns early before focus dispatch)
+- [ ] Alt-tabbing between windows updates the checkmark
+- [ ] Quick-capture toggle does not cause unnecessary menu rebuilds
+- [ ] No visible menu flicker during normal use

 ## Tests
 - [ ] Manual: launch app → Window menu lists main window with checkmark
 - [ ] Manual: Cmd+N → new window appears in Window menu
-- [ ] Manual: close secondary window → removed from Window menu  
-- [ ] Manual: click a task card → Edit menu items update names/enabled state
-- [ ] Manual: quit and relaunch with multiple windows → all appear after restore
+- [ ] Manual: close secondary window → removed from Window menu
+- [ ] Manual: quit and relaunch with multiple windows → all restored windows in Window menu
 - [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` — no regressions

@@ -1,53 +1,56 @@
 ## What

-The Window menu's window list is empty after startup and keeps regressing because menu rebuilds are scattered across OS event handlers instead of flowing through the single focus/scope chain system.
+The command palette and context menu already work correctly — they call `list_commands_for_scope` fresh from the scope chain every time they open. The native menu bar does not. It's built once at startup and manually invalidated from scattered call sites, causing the Window menu to be empty and command availability to go stale.
+
+All three surfaces — menu bar, command palette, right-click menu — are the same thing: commands resolved from the scope chain. Two work. The third needs to catch up.

 ## Root cause

-Menu updates are handled by three separate mechanisms with no unifying invariant:
-1. `rebuild_menu` — called ad-hoc from `create_window_impl`, `Destroyed`, `BoardSwitch`, keymap changes
-2. `update_menu_enabled_state` — called after every `dispatch_command_internal`, toggles enabled/disabled only
-3. `update_window_focus_checkmarks` — called from OS `Focused(true)`, flips checkmarks only
+Menu bar updates use three ad-hoc mechanisms instead of the scope chain:
+1. `rebuild_menu` — called from scattered sites (`create_window_impl`, `Destroyed`, `BoardSwitch`, keymap)
+2. `update_menu_enabled_state` — after every dispatch, toggles enabled/disabled only
+3. `update_window_focus_checkmarks` — OS `Focused(true)`, flips checkmarks only

-None of these are tied to the thing that actually determines what the menu should show: **the scope chain**. The scope chain changes on every focus change — clicking a card, a tag, a column, or switching windows. It includes the `window:{label}` root. It determines available commands. The menu should be a pure function of the scope chain.
+None flow from the scope chain. The palette and context menu don't have this problem because they resolve fresh on every open.

 ## Fix

-**`rebuild_menu` should run on `ui.setFocus` — the only event that matters.**
+**The native menu bar should rebuild on `ui.setFocus` — the event that signals the scope chain has changed.**

-In `dispatch_command_internal`, when `ui.setFocus` is dispatched, call `rebuild_menu` instead of relying on `update_menu_enabled_state` alone. This covers every case through one path:
+In `dispatch_command_internal`, when `ui.setFocus` is dispatched, call `rebuild_menu`. This is the same scope chain change that would cause the palette to show different commands if it were open. The menu bar should reflect the same state.

-- **Restore windows**: each restored window gets `set_focus()` → same flow → all windows in menu
-- **Click a card**: `FocusScope` → `setFocus("task:abc")` → `ui.setFocus` → `rebuild_menu` → menu reflects task-scoped commands
-- **Switch windows**: OS focus → frontend re-dispatches its scope chain → `ui.setFocus` → `rebuild_menu` → checkmark moves
-- **Close window**: `Destroyed` handler stays (destroyed windows don't get focus events) → `rebuild_menu` → window removed
+This covers every case through one path:
+- **Startup**: frontend mounts → `ui.setFocus` → `rebuild_menu` → window appears in menu
+- **New window**: `set_focus()` → frontend mounts → `ui.setFocus` → `rebuild_menu`
+- **Restore windows**: last restored window gets `set_focus()` → same flow
+- **Click a card/tag/column**: `FocusScope` → `ui.setFocus` → `rebuild_menu` → menu reflects scoped commands
+- **Switch windows**: frontend re-dispatches scope chain → `ui.setFocus` → `rebuild_menu`
+- **Close window**: `Destroyed` handler stays (no focus event fires) → `rebuild_menu`

 ### Files to modify
-- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` `dispatch_command_internal` — call `rebuild_menu` when `effective_cmd == "ui.setFocus"` (replaces the ad-hoc `update_menu_enabled_state` for this command)
-- `kanban-app/src/main.rs` `Focused(true)` — remove `update_window_focus_checkmarks` call; keep `most_recent_board` bookkeeping only. Menu updates flow through the scope chain, not OS events.

+- `kanban-app/src/main.rs` `Focused(true)` — remove `update_window_focus_checkmarks` call; keep `most_recent_board` bookkeeping only
+- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` `create_window_impl` — remove redundant `rebuild_menu` call (focus event handles it)

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -13,21 +13,32 @@

 None flow from the scope chain. The palette and context menu don't have this problem because they resolve fresh on every open.

+Additionally, when a window gains OS focus (Alt-Tab, clicking), the frontend does NOT re-dispatch its scope chain. The backend's scope chain stays stale from whichever window last had a click interaction.
+
 ## Fix

-**The native menu bar should rebuild on `ui.setFocus` — the event that signals the scope chain has changed.**
+Two changes:

-In `dispatch_command_internal`, when `ui.setFocus` is dispatched, call `rebuild_menu`. This is the same scope chain change that would cause the palette to show different commands if it were open. The menu bar should reflect the same state.
+### 1. Frontend: re-dispatch `ui.setFocus` on window focus

+Add a window focus listener in `entity-focus-context.tsx` (or `App.tsx`) that re-dispatches the current entity focus scope chain when the window gains OS focus. This ensures the backend always knows the scope chain of the active window, not just the last-clicked window.
+
+Use Tauri's `getCurrentWindow().onFocusChanged()` or the browser `window` focus event to detect OS focus gain, then call the existing `invokeFocusChange` with the current focused moniker and registry.
+
+### 2. Backend: `rebuild_menu` on every `ui.setFocus`
+
+In `dispatch_command_internal`, when `effective_cmd == "ui.setFocus"`, call `menu::rebuild_menu(app)`. This replaces all ad-hoc menu update mechanisms with a single trigger tied to the scope chain.
+
 This covers every case through one path:
 - **Startup**: frontend mounts → `ui.setFocus` → `rebuild_menu` → window appears in menu
 - **New window**: `set_focus()` → frontend mounts → `ui.setFocus` → `rebuild_menu`
 - **Restore windows**: last restored window gets `set_focus()` → same flow
 - **Click a card/tag/column**: `FocusScope` → `ui.setFocus` → `rebuild_menu` → menu reflects scoped commands
-- **Switch windows**: frontend re-dispatches scope chain → `ui.setFocus` → `rebuild_menu`
+- **Switch windows**: OS focus → frontend re-dispatches scope chain → `ui.setFocus` → `rebuild_menu`
 - **Close window**: `Destroyed` handler stays (no focus event fires) → `rebuild_menu`

 ### Files to modify

 ## Why this won't regress

-The palette doesn't regress because it resolves fresh every time. The menu bar will now do the same — rebuild on scope chain change. No call sites to remember. You can't interact with a window without focusing something in it, and switching windows re-dispatches automatically.
